title: Derive short task titles from entry with ellipsis and drawer tooltip
brief: Create short titles based on entrry for new task, and titles should fit using ellipsis and maybe a tooltip, if it opens in the sidebar
description:
When a user creates a new task from the New task composer, the raw entry text currently becomes the title source and long entries produce awkward, mid-word-clipped titles (e.g. 'short titles based on entrry for new task, and titles should fit usin...'). This task improves title generation and title display.

1) Title derivation: when a task is created from the composer brief, derive a concise title from the entry text — take the first meaningful line, strip leading imperative verbs (build/create/add, as deriveTaskTitle in src/prdPrompt.ts already does), strip trailing punctuation, and clip to a short length (target ~60 characters max) at a word boundary rather than mid-word, appending a single ellipsis character '…' when clipped. The full entry remains untouched in the task's brief field; only the display title is shortened. AI-generated titles from the PRD flow (extractTitlePatch in src/prdPrompt.ts) should use the same word-boundary clipping helper instead of the current slice(0,69) mid-word cut.

2) Display fit: on board cards (.cardTitle in webview/styles.css, rendered at webview/main.tsx line ~375), long titles must truncate with CSS ellipsis (single line or two-line clamp — follow the existing card visual style) instead of wrapping indefinitely, and the card element must carry a title attribute (native tooltip) with the full title text.

3) Sidebar/drawer: when a task opens in the drawer (the right-hand sidebar, .drawer in webview/styles.css), the title input (.titleInput, webview/main.tsx line ~430) must show ellipsis truncation for overflowing titles when not being edited, and expose the full title via a title attribute tooltip on hover. Editing behavior must be unaffected: focusing the input shows and edits the full value.

Assumption to record in agentNotes: 'sidebar' in the brief refers to the existing task drawer opened from a card, since that is the only sidebar-like surface in the webview.
acceptanceCriteria: Creating a new task from the composer with a long entry produces a title of at most ~60 characters, clipped at a word boundary with a trailing '…', never cutting a word in half
Creating a task with a short entry (under the limit) uses the cleaned entry as-is with no ellipsis appended
The full original entry text is preserved in the task brief and is still used as the PRD source (getPrdSourceBrief behavior unchanged)
deriveTaskTitle and the AI title patch path (extractTitlePatch) share the same word-boundary clipping logic — no remaining mid-word slice(0,69) truncation

Board card titles truncate with an ellipsis instead of overflowing or wrapping beyond the card's visual limit, and hovering a truncated card title shows the full title via a native tooltip (title attribute)
In the task drawer, an overflowing title in the title input renders with ellipsis when unfocused, and hovering it shows the full title as a tooltip
Focusing the drawer title input allows viewing and editing the complete title text normally
Existing tasks with saved titles are unaffected except for display truncation — no stored titles are rewritten on load beyond current behavior
Unit tests in tests/prdPrompt.test.mjs cover word-boundary clipping: long entry, entry exactly at the limit, entry with a very long first word, and multi-line entry using only the first line
qaChecklist: Create a task with a 200+ character single-line entry and verify the generated title ends at a word boundary with '…' and is ≤ ~60 characters
Create a task with a multi-line entry and verify only the first non-empty line feeds the title
Create a task whose entry starts with 'Create ' or 'Build ' and verify the verb is stripped from the title
Open a task with a long title in the drawer and confirm the title shows ellipsis when unfocused and the full text on focus
Hover the truncated title on a board card and in the drawer and confirm a tooltip shows the complete title
Verify the brief field in .agent-board/tasks/<id>.json still contains the full original entry
Run Generate PRD on a task and confirm the AI-supplied title is also word-boundary clipped, not cut mid-word
Edit a title manually in the drawer to something long, save, and confirm the card truncates it correctly without altering the stored value
designQaChecklist: Card title truncation matches the existing card typography (13px, weight 500) and does not change card height rhythm
Ellipsis uses the single '…' character, not three periods, in generated titles
Drawer title input keeps its 17px semibold styling; truncation only appears when the text overflows
Tooltip appears only when the title is actually truncated where feasible, or is harmless (identical text) when not
No layout shift in the drawer header or card when toggling between truncated and focused/full states
constraints: Follow existing project patterns before introducing new abstractions — extend deriveTaskTitle/extractTitlePatch in src/prdPrompt.ts rather than adding a new title module
Keep changes scoped to title derivation and title display; do not modify PRD generation content, task schema, or unrelated drawer fields
Do not overwrite unrelated user or agent changes; do not rewrite stored titles of existing tasks
Do not alter the brief field — it is the PRD source of truth
Use the CSS variables and class conventions already present in webview/styles.css; no new styling frameworks
VS Code webview context: rely on native title-attribute tooltips (window.confirm-style APIs are blocked; custom tooltip libraries are unnecessary)
agentNotes:
Implementation (branch agent-board/TASK-011-derive-short-task-titles-from-entry-with-ellipsi):
- src/prdPrompt.ts: new exported clipTitle(text, maxLength = TITLE_MAX_LENGTH(60)) clips at the last word boundary within the budget, strips trailing punctuation, appends a single U+2026 ellipsis, and hard-clips only when the first word alone exceeds the limit; result including the ellipsis never exceeds 60 chars. deriveTaskTitle (composer path) and normalizeSpecPatch (AI title path — the task description calls it extractTitlePatch; normalizeSpecPatch is where the title patch logic lives) both use it; the old slice(0,69)+"..." cuts are gone. The PRD prompt title instruction now says max 60 to match.
- webview/main.tsx: card title span and drawer title input carry title attributes (native tooltips, harmless when not truncated). webview/styles.css: .cardTitle uses a two-line -webkit-line-clamp with the same 13px/500 typography; .titleInput gets text-overflow: ellipsis when unfocused and clip on focus so editing shows the full value.
- tests/prdPrompt.test.mjs: updated the legacy 72-char assertion and added cases for word-boundary clip on a long multi-word entry, entry exactly at the 60 limit, first word longer than the limit, multi-line first-line-only derivation, short entry pass-through, and the composer-path long brief.
Assumption: sidebar in the brief means the task drawer, the only sidebar-like surface in the webview. Stored titles are never rewritten on load; clipping applies at derivation/generation time and display truncation is CSS-only.

qaNotes.message: QA caveat: performed by the implementing agent (no other agent free). Logic verified with tests plus fresh-bundle edge probes; hover/tooltip behavior verified by code review — recommend a quick human hover check on a long-titled card.
QA passed: validation green, required unit tests plus adversarial edge probes all pass, stored titles verified untouched.

qaEvidence: Re-ran run-validation.mjs during QA: typecheck, compile, test, build all PASS.

Unit tests cover the four required cases (long entry word-boundary, exact 60-char limit, over-long first word, multi-line first-line-only) plus short-entry pass-through and the composer-path brief; all green.

Adversarial edge probes on a fresh bundle: 61-char single word hard-clips to exactly 60 incl ellipsis; space-at-59 clips on the boundary; punctuation before the boundary is stripped before the ellipsis; accented unicode text clips on whole words; empty input returns empty (deriveTaskTitle falls back to task id); short punctuation-only input passes through.
Stored-title safety verified: deriveTaskTitle returns an existing title untouched even when longer than 60 chars — no rewriting on load.
Both slice(0,69) mid-word cuts removed; deriveTaskTitle and normalizeSpecPatch share clipTitle; generated ellipsis is the single U+2026 character (asserted in tests).

Display: card title uses a two-line -webkit-line-clamp keeping 13px/500 typography; card span and drawer title input carry native title tooltips; input shows ellipsis only when unfocused (text-overflow: clip on focus), so editing is unaffected and there is no layout shift.
